mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates tightening supply capacity and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of contraction months during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows zero predictive power in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >60% of months where the rate threshold is breached, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which stimulates downstream economic growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following housing starts increases, or the series shows no significant correlation with equity returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 110 signals strong manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in >60% of triggered months during recovery phases, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy in regime-specific walk-forward tests.
